question: The police are searching for the primary ________ who is believed to have committed the robbery.
explanation: A "suspect" is the person the police think did the crime.

question: After two weeks of hearing evidence, the jury finally returned a ________ of guilty.
explanation: A "verdict" is the official decision made by a jury at the end of a court trial.

question: Because the patient was a child, she was admitted to the ________ department for specialized treatment.
explanation: "Paediatrics" is the specific area of medicine that looks after children.

question: The bank teller warned the customer that the five-thousand-kwacha note was ________ and had no value.
explanation: "Counterfeit" means that money is fake and not real.

question: The meeting could not start because the members present did not form a ________.
explanation: A "quorum" is the lowest number of people needed for a meeting to be official.

question: During the trial, the witness was warned that giving false information under oath constitutes ________.
explanation: "Perjury" is the crime of telling lies in a court of law.

question: The doctor wrote a ________ for the patient to collect the required antibiotics from the chemist.
explanation: A "prescription" is a special note from a doctor for medicine.

question: Most people in the church had to stand because all the ________ were already occupied.
explanation: "Pews" are the long wooden seats found in a church.

question: The editor asked the reporter to ________ the story to ensure it met the newspaper’s standards before printing.
explanation: To "edit" a story means to check it and make changes to make it better.

question: The actors were nervous during the ________ because the director was looking for specific skills for the new play.
explanation: An "audition" is a test where actors show their skills to get a job in a play.

question: To ensure the safety of the house, the electrician installed a new ________ to prevent fire from power surges.
explanation: A "fuse" is a safety part that stops electricity if it becomes dangerous.

question: The two warring nations finally agreed on a ________ to stop fighting and allow for peace talks.
explanation: A "truce" is an agreement between enemies to stop fighting for a short time.

question: The company was forced to declare itself ________ after incurring more debts than it could possibly repay.
explanation: "Bankrupt" means a person or business has no money to pay what they owe.

question: The specialist performed a small ________ on the patient’s arm to remove the infected tissue.
explanation: An "incision" is a clean cut made by a doctor during a medical operation.

question: Before the parliamentary debate began, the secretary read out the ________ of the previous meeting.
explanation: "Minutes" are the written notes that record everything said at a previous meeting.

question: The high court decided to ________ the prisoner after proving that he was not at the scene of the crime.
explanation: To "acquit" someone means the court says they are not guilty of a crime.

question: Each month, the insurance company requires me to pay a ________ to keep my policy active.
explanation: A "premium" is the regular payment you make for insurance.

question: The singer’s ________ album was a great success, selling thousands of copies in its first week.
explanation: A "debut" is the very first time an artist releases their work to the public.

question: Because there was inadequate electrical power, the company introduced ________ to control the supply.
explanation: "Load shedding" is when the power is turned off in some areas to save electricity.

question: The ________ made a legal complaint against the driver for damaging his fence.
explanation: A "plaintiff" is the person who starts a case in court against someone else.

question: After the accident, the body was taken to the lab for a ________ to establish the cause of death.
explanation: A "postmortem" is an exam of a dead body to find out why they died.

question: The priest stood on the ________ to deliver a powerful sermon to the congregation.
explanation: A "pulpit" is the high platform in a church where the priest stands to talk.

question: I receive a monthly bank ________ so that I can monitor all the transactions in my account.
explanation: A "statement" is a monthly report from the bank about your money.

question: The young man was charged with ________ for killing the pedestrian unlawfully but without prior intention.
explanation: "Manslaughter" is killing someone by accident or without planning to do it.

question: The government sent a special ________ to represent the country at the international climate summit.
explanation: A "delegate" is a person chosen to represent their group or country at a big meeting.

question: The patient is ________ and is gradually regaining strength after a long battle with pneumonia.
explanation: A "convalescent" person is someone who is slowly getting better after being very sick.

question: The author has submitted the ________ of his new novel to the publishers for consideration.
explanation: A "manuscript" is the original copy of a story before it is made into a book.

question: The judge decided to give the offender a ________, meaning he was set free but must not commit another crime for a year.
explanation: A "suspended sentence" means you are free unless you break the law again.

question: To help the actor who had forgotten his lines, the stage manager provided several ________ from the wings.
explanation: "Prompts" are whispered words given to actors to help them remember what to say.

question: The manager was accused of ________ after he accepted money to influence his decision on the new contract.
explanation: "Bribery" is the crime of giving or taking money to get a special favor.

question: The manager was accused of ________ after he accepted money to influence his decision on the new contract.
explanation: Bribery is the crime of giving or taking money to get a special favor from someone in power.

question: The ________ represents a specific area where voters choose their Member of Parliament.
explanation: A constituency is the specific area or land where people live and vote for their representative.

question: The victim of the fire received ________ from the government to help him rebuild his house.
explanation: Compensation is money given to someone to make up for a loss, a hurt, or damage.

question: During the Christian wedding ceremony, the bride’s father ________ her to her new husband.
explanation: In a wedding, "gave away" is the traditional term for when a father walks his daughter to the groom.

question: The nurse used a ________ to listen to the patient’s heartbeat and breathing.
explanation: A stethoscope is the tool doctors and nurses use to hear the sounds inside a person's body.

question: The abductors are demanding a huge ________ before they can release the kidnapped child.
explanation: A ransom is the money that criminals demand before they will set a captured person free.

question: Many young people today suffer from ________, showing a total lack of interest in participating in national elections.
explanation: Apathy means that a person does not care or has no interest in important things like voting.

question: The company’s ________ have increased significantly because they are paying more for raw materials than they are earning.
explanation: Debts are the amounts of money that a person or a company owes to others.

question: The priest was officially ________ as a Bishop during the colourful ceremony held at the cathedral.
explanation: Consecrated is the official religious word used when someone is made a Bishop.

question: The doctor’s ________ showed that the patient was suffering from a rare type of tropical fever.
explanation: A diagnosis is the doctor’s official decision about what sickness a patient has.

question: The ________ list of items to be discussed at the board meeting was sent to all directors in advance.
explanation: An agenda is a list that shows all the things people will talk about during a meeting.

question: The ________ is a person who is currently on trial for allegedly stealing company funds.
explanation: The accused is the person who is charged with a crime and is standing trial in court.

question: In a business transaction, the person to whom money is paid is formally referred to as the ________.
explanation: A payee is the person who receives money in a business deal or on a check.

question: The ________ was commended for his bravery after he was held by the enemy for six months during the war.
explanation: A hostage is a person who is caught and kept as a prisoner by an enemy.

question: The play was divided into three ________, each containing several scenes.
explanation: Acts are the large parts that a play is divided into.

question: The ________ is a statement of the money paid to a worker every week for manual or physical work.
explanation: Wages are the money paid to a worker every week, usually for physical labor.

question: After the massive storm, the town suffered a total ________, and no one had electricity for two days.
explanation: A black out is when all the lights and power in an area go out at once.

question: The lawyer began the ________ by asking the witness very detailed questions to check if his story was true.
explanation: Cross-examination is when a lawyer asks the other side's witness questions to see if they are lying.

question: At the cemetery, the family gathered around the ________ to pay their final respects to the deceased.
explanation: A tomb is the place where a dead person is buried.

question: The government offered ________ to all political prisoners who were willing to return home peacefully.
explanation: Amnesty is an official pardon or forgiveness given by the government to a group of people.

question: The ________ are the clothes and materials used by actors to look like the characters they are playing.
explanation: Costumes are the special clothes actors wear so they can look like the person in the story.
